title: Differentiating neural systems mediating the acquisition vs. expression of goal-directed and habitual behavioral control.

assets.abstract: Considerable behavioral data indicate that operant actions can become habitual, as demonstrated by insensitivity to changes in the action-outcome contingency and in subjective outcome values. Notably, although several studies have investigated the neural substrates of habits, none has clearly differentiated the areas of the human brain that support habit formation from those that implement habitual control. phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
